description_long: This stone is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized rock with a rugged, natural appearance. Its surface is predominantly gray with patches of lighter, almost white, rounded inclusions that give it a conglomerate texture. One side of the stone is notably smoother and flatter, suggesting it may have been fractured or cut, while the other sides are rough and pitted with visible protrusions and indentations. The stone's overall form is asymmetrical, with a somewhat oval outline when viewed from above. The weathering on the stone is evident, with some areas appearing more eroded and others retaining sharper edges. This type of stone could commonly be found in urban parks, used as a decorative or functional barrier.
description: A rugged, gray stone with white inclusions and a mix of rough and smooth surfaces, featuring an irregular, asymmetrical shape.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's rough, pitted surface with prominent white inclusions and a generally rounded, uneven profile.
description_view_1: This side shows a smoother, flatter surface, likely the result of a natural fracture or cut, with minimal inclusions visible.
description_view_2: From above, the stone appears oval and flat, emphasizing its broad, smooth surface and subtle color variations.
description_view_3: This angle reveals a combination of rough, conglomerate texture and a more solid, rounded end, with visible layering and inclusions.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier along a path
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with rounded white inclusions and patches, giving a conglomerate appearance with no regular pattern.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with visible signs of erosion and some smoother, possibly fractured surfaces, indicating moderate weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but can blend with natural landscaping elements; may be partially surrounded by grass or mulch.
